blocks.content: Friday dinner at the Jones house. The table was set the way it always was — extra plate for Kit, sweet tea already poured, Mrs. Jones moving between the kitchen and the dining room.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Kit Jenkins. You look thin.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Mrs. Jones, I'm the same as I was at Christmas.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: California doesn't feed people right. Sit down.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She's been saying that since you left in August.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Have you been defending me.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I've been letting her worry. It's her hobby.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: I can hear you both.
Dinner was pot roast and biscuits and sweet tea and exactly what Georgia in May felt like. Kit ate two plates. Mrs. Jones looked satisfied about it.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Daisy tells me your film screened in a class.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Professor used it as an example. I'm still not totally sure what kind.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: The good kind. I can tell by how you said it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: That's what I told him.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She said it had emotional architecture.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Well. That sounds like a compliment to me.
After dinner, Daisy walked him to the porch. The night was warm — Georgia warm. Fireflies had already started at the edges of the yard.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She's going to feed you every week this summer.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm not complaining.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know you're not.
He stood at the edge of the porch steps. She leaned against the post. The yard between them was full of soft light.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It's good to be home.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It is.
